metadata.extraction_coverage: All non-HT-Wa entries fully extracted. HT tablets (HT 1-154G) fully extracted. HT Wa sealings (861 entries) require batch OCR — structure documented but individual rows pending. KH Wc roundels partially extracted.

columns.inventory_number: Museum inventory number (HM = Heraklion Museum, AM = Ashmolean Museum, A. Nik. M. = Agios Nikolaos Museum, NM = National Museum Athens, MSMA = Musée Stratigraphique de Mallia, etc.)
columns.dating: Archaeological dating (MM = Middle Minoan, MR = Late Minoan [Minoen Récent], I/II/III = period, A/B = subperiod)
columns.scribe: Scribe identification by L. Godart (number + site code, e.g. '9 HT' = scribe 9 at Hagia Triada)
columns.gorila_ref: Volume and page in GORILA publication

metadata.ht_wa_sealings_note: The HT Wa entries (Hagia Triada sealings, ~861 entries from HT Wa 1001 to HT Wa 3025+) are the largest single block in the concordance, spanning ~15 pages. Each entry maps a sealing number to an HM inventory number. These require systematic batch extraction from pages 139-155 of Vol 5. The inventory numbers follow patterns like HM 401.1, HM 401.2, etc.
